excerpt: ATSDR p.48 gives Hiroshima a resident civilian base of about 250,000 for the bombing-day context.
confidence_note: Strong for resident civilians only; excludes unrecorded military personnel and foreign workers.

excerpt: ATSDR p.48 reports 45,000 first-day deaths in Hiroshima against the resident-civilian denominator.
transform_note: Used as the baseline Hiroshima civilian direct-death series because it isolates first-day deaths from later months.
confidence_note: Strong for resident-civilian first-day deaths; likely omits some nonresident civilians and foreign workers.

excerpt: ATSDR separately lists 19,000 additional Hiroshima deaths over the subsequent four months.
transform_note: Excluded from core DR because the source does not separate delayed radiation, burn-complication, and other later causes into a clean direct-only series.
confidence_note: Useful context only; not baseline DR input.

excerpt: ATSDR warns that actual totals may be higher because military personnel and foreign workers were under-recorded.
transform_note: Supports leaving unknown-status direct deaths unquantified rather than allocating by subtraction.
confidence_note: Important undercount warning; no usable numeric split.

excerpt: HyperWar/AAF Vol. V gives 24,158 soldiers in Hiroshima for the military-relief context after the blast.
confidence_note: Best accessible explicit Hiroshima military-presence count, but still part of a narrative reconstruction rather than a published roster.

excerpt: HyperWar/AAF Vol. V reports 6,769 Hiroshima soldiers killed or missing because of the bombing.
transform_note: Treated as direct attack deaths for the Hiroshima baseline. A later secondary retelling gives 6,789, but the official-history web text says 6,769.
confidence_note: Strongest explicit Hiroshima military-death figure in accessible source set; still some reporting variance in later summaries.

excerpt: DOE/OSTI gives Hiroshima a civilian population of almost 300,000 on the bombing day.
transform_note: Used only as a high civilian exposure bound; not preferred over the matched ATSDR resident-civilian denominator.
confidence_note: Round and likely inclusive of more civilian-present categories than the ATSDR resident-civilian figure.

excerpt: DOE/OSTI describes Hiroshima as containing about 43,000 soldiers on 1945-08-06.
transform_note: Used only as a high military exposure bound to reflect broader day-of military presence than the HyperWar count.
confidence_note: Round and not tied to a transparent military-roll methodology.

excerpt: Hiroshima Prefecture says about 350,000 people were in Hiroshima, including inhabitants, military personnel, and commuters.
transform_note: Context only; too broad and mixed for direct civilian denominator use.
confidence_note: Useful for explaining why end-1945 totals and broader population counts exceed resident-civilian baselines.

excerpt: Hiroshima Prefecture gives an end-1945 death estimate of about 140,000 with a ±10,000 range.
transform_note: Excluded from baseline DR because the time horizon mixes direct deaths with later radiation and burn-complication mortality.
confidence_note: Important context figure, but not a same-day direct-death estimate.

excerpt: Wellerstein reports a Joint Commission estimate that around 40,000-50,000 died at Hiroshima on the first day.
transform_note: Used only to widen the Hiroshima first-day uncertainty band around the ATSDR resident-civilian point estimate.
confidence_note: Useful for same-day bounding, but it is an all-status first-day context figure rather than a pure civilian series.

excerpt: Yale/Avalon reproduces an early U.S. total of 66,000 dead for Hiroshima from a 255,000 pre-raid base.
transform_note: Context only; not direct-only and not status-split.
confidence_note: Shows the lower early American reconstruction against which later Japanese-led totals are often compared.

excerpt: ATSDR uses a Nagasaki resident-population base of about 174,000 for the first-day death estimate.
transform_note: Treated as the matched Nagasaki civilian denominator because the same sentence warns that military personnel and foreign workers were under-recorded.
confidence_note: Good matched base for first-day deaths, but civilian status is inferred from context rather than explicitly stated.

excerpt: ATSDR gives Nagasaki 22,000 first-day deaths against the same 174,000 resident-population base.
transform_note: Used as the baseline Nagasaki civilian first-day direct-death estimate.
confidence_note: Best accessible matched first-day Nagasaki series, though status is inferred from context.

excerpt: ATSDR separately lists 17,000 additional Nagasaki deaths within the subsequent four months.
transform_note: Excluded from core DR because later-month deaths are not separated into clean direct versus delayed causes.
confidence_note: Context only; not baseline DR input.

excerpt: ATSDR warns that Nagasaki actual totals may be higher because military personnel and foreign workers were under-recorded.
transform_note: Supports leaving unknown-status direct deaths unquantified rather than allocating residual deaths mechanically.
confidence_note: Important undercount warning; no usable numeric split.

excerpt: Nagasaki City's peace site states the city population was about 240,000 when the bomb was dropped.
pages_or_sections: Official page search snippet / official leaflet wording
transform_note: Used as a wider civilian exposure sensitivity bound, not as the matched baseline denominator.
confidence_note: Official and useful, but it is broader than the matched ATSDR resident-population base.

excerpt: Nagasaki City's peace site gives 73,884 deaths by the end of December 1945.
pages_or_sections: Official page search snippet / official leaflet wording
transform_note: Excluded from baseline DR because it mixes immediate and delayed mortality.
confidence_note: Important context figure, but not a same-day direct-death estimate.

excerpt: The Nuclear Museum page says 263,000 people were in Nagasaki, including residents, soldiers, and POWs.
transform_note: Context only; too mixed for direct use as the civilian denominator.
confidence_note: Helpful for composition, but not a clean exposure base.

excerpt: The Nuclear Museum page explicitly includes 9,000 Japanese soldiers in Nagasaki on the bombing day.
confidence_note: Best quickly accessible Nagasaki military-presence figure in the searched source set.

excerpt: The Nuclear Museum page gives a broad immediate-death range of 40,000 to 75,000 for Nagasaki.
transform_note: Not used in the core DR path because it is broad, all-status, and not cleanly matched to the resident-civilian denominator.
confidence_note: Useful only as a broad contextual ceiling around the city-wide immediate toll.

excerpt: A tertiary but specific compilation reports that 150 Japanese soldiers were killed instantly in Nagasaki.
transform_note: Used narrowly and down-weighted. Replace with a primary or archival source if this bundle is revised.
confidence_note: Low-to-moderate confidence because the source is tertiary.

excerpt: Yale/Avalon reproduces an early U.S. total of 39,000 dead for Nagasaki from a 195,000 pre-raid base.
transform_note: Context only; not first-day direct-only and not status-split.
confidence_note: Useful comparison figure for understanding why early American totals fall below later Japanese end-1945 totals.
